RADIOLOGY REPORT

EXAM: ULTRASOUND ABDOMEN COMPLETE
Date: 09/10/2024

CLINICAL HISTORY: Abdominal pain, history of cholecystectomy.
Evaluate for biliary or hepatic pathology.

TECHNIQUE: Real-time grayscale and color Doppler sonographic
evaluation of the abdomen.

FINDINGS:

Liver: Normal in size, measuring 14.2 cm in length. Homogeneous
echotexture. No focal hepatic lesion. No intrahepatic biliary
ductal dilatation.

Gallbladder: Surgically absent (status post cholecystectomy).

Common bile duct: Measures 4 mm in diameter, within normal limits
for post-cholecystectomy patient (up to 10mm considered normal
post-cholecystectomy).

Pancreas: Head and body are visualized and appear unremarkable.
Tail is partially obscured by overlying bowel gas.

Right kidney: 10.8 cm. Normal cortical thickness. No hydronephrosis
or nephrolithiasis. Normal corticomedullary differentiation.

Left kidney: 11.1 cm. Normal cortical thickness. No hydronephrosis
or nephrolithiasis.

Spleen: Normal in size at 10.5 cm. Homogeneous echotexture.

Aorta: Visualized portions are normal in caliber. No aneurysm.

No free fluid in the abdomen or pelvis.

IMPRESSION:
1. No acute abdominal abnormality.
2. Status post cholecystectomy. Bile duct normal caliber.
3. No hepatic lesion or biliary obstruction.

Dictated by: DR. NGUYEN, LISA (RADIOLOGY)
Electronically signed: 09/10/2024 1545
